rule.statement: Each ceremony MUST have a one-sentence purpose published in the config; purpose-less ceremonies are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without published purpose, ceremonies degrade into rituals.

rule.statement: Each ceremony MUST have a published time-box that the facilitator enforces; over-runs require explicit retro action.
rule.rationale: Time-boxes are the discipline that makes Scrum work.

rule.statement: Every retro MUST produce ≥1 action item with named owner + due-date; closure is tracked next retro.
rule.rationale: Retros without actions are theatre; closure tracking is what produces compounding improvement.

rule.statement: Total ceremony hours per sprint MUST be < 10% of sprint capacity; over-10% triggers retune.
rule.rationale: Above 10%, ceremonies pay less than they cost.

rule.statement: Each ceremony MUST list mandatory + optional attendees; open-attendance ceremonies are rejected.
rule.rationale: Open invites bloat ceremonies and dilute decisions.
